workHistory.summary: Building new product design engineering teams to design the next generations of AI Glasses hardware at Reality Labs.
highlights: Standing up new product design engineering teams focused on next-generation AI Glasses hardware.
Setting team direction, hiring bar, and engineering rigor for a fast-growing PD organization inside Reality Labs.
Partnering across product, industrial design, systems engineering, and manufacturing to translate ambitious AR concepts into shippable consumer hardware.
Coaching senior individual contributors and tech leads on architecture decisions, cross-functional scope, and engineering judgment.

workHistory.summary: Technical lead on Reality Labs hardware — designing the future of AI Glasses, with prior work on wrist wearables.
highlights: Led the design of the metal enclosure for a first-generation, mass-market AR wearable device.
Drove bold design and architecture changes to resolve complex cross-functional, system-level issues.
Helped set up the processes, tools, and engineering rigor of a new and rapidly growing product design org.
Transitioned from wrist wearables into AI Glasses work, carrying forward learnings on consumer-scale wearable hardware.

workHistory.summary: Stood up Lime's product design engineering function and led vehicle architecture across multiple EV platforms during a critical hardware-team buildout.
highlights: Built a world-class hardware team from the ground up to deliver high-quality EVs.
Implemented the processes and tooling to set the new PD team up for success — from PLM systems to a hardware-specific facility.
Led vehicle architecture definition across several hardware platforms in different vehicle categories.
Partnered with cross-functional teams to model the business-metric impact of hardware architecture decisions globally, enabling data-driven design choices.

workHistory.summary: Owned vehicle-level architecture and subsystem design across Boosted's e-scooter and next-generation vehicle programs.
highlights: Led the vehicle architecture and product design of Boosted's next vehicle platform — a new form-factor category and the company's most complex and ambitious vehicle to date.
Defined vehicle architecture, engineering and product requirements, and core technologies from the concept phase in close collaboration with product, industrial design, and regulatory.
Built and led a team of 6–8 engineers shipping rideable prototypes, integrating components, and exploring new technologies.
Owned the handlebar assembly of the 25 mph Boosted Rev scooter — including throttle, display, and structural components — from ID concept to mass production in China in 10 months to PVT.
Designed parts for overmolding, ultrasonic welding, forging, precision CNC, and water-ingress protection; sourced and managed full-time engineering contractors to fill gaps in specialist skill sets.

workHistory.role: Technical Lead, Mechanical Design Engineer (also Technical Project Manager)
workHistory.summary: Owned the stack assembly — one of two major subsystems of the 125 kWh EnergyPod flow battery — from R&D and prototyping through supplier development and high-volume manufacturing.
highlights: Owned the design of the stack assembly (~100 unique part numbers, 4,000+ total parts) representing the majority of the battery's IP and over half of its BOM cost.
Reduced stack-assembly cost 25–35% each release phase by introducing high-volume manufacturing processes and qualifying new suppliers across the US and Asia.
Performed DFM and supplier qualification across laser welding, injection molding, progressive die stamping, and surface texturing.

Stepped into a Technical Project Manager role for the battery's market introduction — planning engineering phases, driving supplier ramp, and defining release configurations — while continuing core design engineering work.
Earlier in the role, redesigned the electrode assembly for the 2nd-generation battery from whiteboard concepts, reducing cost ~85% while improving storage capacity and scalability.

workHistory.summary: Summer internships across four years at a motorcycle engineering firm behind the first all-electric motorcycle, the first carbon-fiber dirt bike, and work for Husqvarna, Aprilia, and Honda.
highlights: Tuned motorcycle handling by modifying fundamental chassis geometries.
Finalized the design, assembled components, and tested the Aprilia ETV 1000 Mana prototype.
Assisted in the development and testing of the Vectrix VX-1 — the first street-legal all-electric motorcycle — and the SCAM Light Tactical Vehicle truck.

education.institution: California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo
education.details: Graduate research on Scheffler solar concentrator: designed, built, and tested a new solar concentrator with a target production cost of $100; coded dynamic and optic simulations in MATLAB; manufactured 2m² composite solar reflectors using CNC-cut foam molds.

education.institution: California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo
education.details: Propulsion Integration Lead, AIAA Undergraduate Aircraft Design Competition — 2nd place nationally. Designed a 737/A320-replacement aircraft with an original three-engine boundary-layer-ingestion configuration; presented at Boeing Commercial & Phantom Works, Northrop Grumman, and Edwards AFB.

education.institution: California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo
education.credential: B.S. Physics (all upper-division coursework, senior project not completed)
education.details: Concurrent physics coursework alongside aerospace and mechanical engineering programs.
K–12 at the American School of Milan; bilingual English / Italian environment.
